---
title: "London ULEZ Economic & Environmental Dashboard"
output:
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    vertical_layout: scroll
theme: cosmo
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(lubridate)
library(readr)

# Load datasets
business_data <- read.csv('business-demographics.csv')
job_density <- read.csv('jobs-and-job-density.csv')
workforce_jobs <- read.csv('workforce-jobs.csv')
workforce_sector <- read.csv('workforce-jobs-by-sector.csv')



```



---

# **Full Dashboard**

Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Active Enterprises in London
```{r}


### Active Enterprises


business_data <- read.csv('business-demographics.csv')

business_london <- business_data %>%
  filter(year >= 2015 & year <= 2023, area == "London")

business_summary <- business_london %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_active_enterprises = sum(active_enterprises, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_births = sum(births, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_deaths = sum(deaths, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(year = 2023, total_active_enterprises = 592330, total_births = 74650, total_deaths = 65175),
    tibble(year = 2024, total_active_enterprises = 595280, total_births = 75550, total_deaths = 61245)
  )

max_y <- max(business_summary$total_active_enterprises)
label_y <- max_y * 1.003

p_active <- ggplot(business_summary, aes(year, total_active_enterprises)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019, linetype="dashed") +
  annotate("text", x=2019, y=label_y, label="ULEZ introduced", angle=90, hjust=0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2021, linetype="dashed", colour="darkgreen") +
  annotate("text", x=2021, y=label_y, label="ULEZ Inner EXP", angle=90, hjust=0, colour="darkgreen") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2023, linetype="dashed", colour="purple") +
  annotate("text", x=2023, y=label_y, label="ULEZ Greater EXP", angle=90, hjust=0, colour="purple") +
  annotate("text", x=2020.5, y=label_y*1.01, label="COVID-19 period") +
  geom_line(colour="#1f78b4", linewidth=1.2) +
  geom_point(colour="#1f78b4", size=2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks=2015:2024) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits=c(520000, 625000),
    breaks=seq(520000, 625000, 20000),
    labels=scales::label_number(scale=1e-3, suffix="k")
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
ggplotly(p_active)

```

### Business Births vs Deaths

```{r}

business_summary$year <- as.numeric(business_summary$year)

p_birth_death<- ggplot(business_summary, aes(x = year)) +
  
  # COVID shaded period
  geom_rect(
    aes(
      xmin = 2020,
      xmax = 2021,
      ymin = -Inf,
      ymax = Inf
    ),
    fill = "grey50",
    alpha = 0.25,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ vertical line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2019,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2019,
    y = max(business_summary$total_births, na.rm = TRUE),
    label = "ULEZ introduced",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2021 vertical line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2021,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "darkgreen",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2021,
    y = max(business_summary$total_births, na.rm = TRUE),
    label = "ULEZ Inner EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2023 vertical line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2023,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "purple",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +
  
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2023,
    y = max(business_summary$total_births, na.rm = TRUE),
    label = "ULEZ Greater EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  
  
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2020.5,
    y = max(business_summary$total_births, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.95,
    label = "COVID-19 period",
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  
  geom_line(aes(y = total_births, colour = "Births"), linewidth = 1.1) +
  geom_line(aes(y = total_deaths, colour = "Deaths"), linewidth = 1.1) +
  
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Births" = "green",
    "Deaths" = "red"
  )) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2015, 2024, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Business Births and Deaths in London (2015–2024)",
    subtitle = "ULEZ introduction and COVID-19 period highlighted",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Business Births / Deaths",
    colour = "Indicator"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(p_birth_death)
```



### Job Density

```{r}

job_density_long <- job_density %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("X"),
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "job_density"
  ) %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(sub("X", "", year)))

job_density_filtered <- job_density_long %>%
  filter(year >= 2016 & year <= 2023)



job_density_long <- job_density %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("X"),
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "job_density"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(sub("X", "", year)),
    job_density = parse_number(job_density)
  )


job_density_filtered <- job_density_long %>%
  filter(year >= 2016 & year <= 2023)

city_data <- job_density_filtered %>%
  filter(Area == "London")


library(plotly)

max_y <- max(city_data$job_density, na.rm = TRUE)

p_job_density <- ggplot(city_data, aes(x = year, y = job_density)) +
  
  # COVID shading
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = 2019.8,
    xmax = 2021.2,
    ymin = -Inf,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = "#bdbdbd",
    alpha = 0.35
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2019
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2019,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "black",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2019,
    y = max_y,
    label = "ULEZ introduced",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2021 expansion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2021,      
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "darkgreen",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2021,
    y = max_y,
    label = "ULEZ Inner EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "darkgreen"
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2023 expansion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2023,      
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "purple",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2023.1,                
    y = max_y * 1.005,         
    label = "ULEZ Greater EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "purple"
  )+


  
  # COVID label
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2020.5,
    y = max_y * 0.9,
    label = "COVID-19\n(2020–2021)",
    size = 3.6
  ) +
  
  geom_line(colour = "blue", linewidth = 1.3) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Job Density in London (2016–2023)",
    subtitle = "ULEZ introduction and COVID-19 period highlighted",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Jobs per working-age resident"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(p_job_density)

```



### Workforce Jobs in London
```{r}

workforce_jobs <- workforce_jobs %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(ymd(paste0(date, "-01"))),
    quarter = quarter(ymd(paste0(date, "-01")))
  )

workforce_london <- workforce_jobs %>%
  filter(area == "London", year >= 2016 & year <= 2024)

workforce_summary <- workforce_london %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    total_jobs = mean(total.workforce.jobs, na.rm = TRUE),
    employee_jobs = mean(employee.jobs, na.rm = TRUE),
    self_employment_jobs = mean(self.employment.jobs, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

max_y <- max(
  workforce_summary$total_jobs,
  workforce_summary$employee_jobs,
  workforce_summary$self_employment_jobs,
  na.rm = TRUE
)

p_workforce <- ggplot(workforce_summary, aes(x = year)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin = 2019.8, xmax = 2021.2, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.35) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019, linetype = "dashed") +
  annotate("text", x = 2020.5, y = max_y * 0.68,
           label = "COVID-19\n(2020–2021)", size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 2019, y = max_y, label = "ULEZ introduced",
           angle = 90, vjust = -0.4, size = 3.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2021, linetype = "dashed", colour = "darkgreen") +
  annotate("text", x = 2021, y = max_y, label = "ULEZ Inner EXP",
           angle = 90, vjust = -0.4, size = 3.4, colour = "darkgreen") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2023, linetype = "dashed", colour = "purple") +
  annotate("text", x = 2023.1, y = max_y * 1.005, label = "ULEZ Greater EXP",
           angle = 90, vjust = -0.4, size = 3.4, colour = "purple") +
  geom_line(aes(y = total_jobs, colour = "Total jobs"), linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = employee_jobs, colour = "Employee jobs"), linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = self_employment_jobs, colour = "Self-employed jobs"), linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Total jobs" = "#1b9e77",
    "Employee jobs" = "#7570b3",
    "Self-employed jobs" = "#d95f02"
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(
    title = "Workforce Jobs in London (2016–2024)",
    subtitle = "Impact of ULEZ introduction and COVID-19 period",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Jobs",
    colour = "Job Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

ggplotly(p_workforce)

```




Row
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r, fig.height=6}

# Filter data
workforce_sector_filtered <- workforce_sector %>%
  filter(year >= 2016 & year <= 2023)

key_sectors <- c(
  "Construction",
  "Accommodation and food service activities",
  "Transportation and storage",
  "Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles",
  "Professional, scientific and technical activities"
)

sector_plot_data <- workforce_sector_filtered %>%
  filter(industries %in% key_sectors)

# OPTIONAL: shorten labels (cleaner legend)
sector_plot_data$industries <- recode(sector_plot_data$industries,
  "Accommodation and food service activities" = "Hospitality",
  "Professional, scientific and technical activities" = "Professional",
  "Transportation and storage" = "Transport",
  "Wholesale and retail trade; repair of motor vehicles and motorcycles" = "Retail",
  "Construction" = "Construction"
)

# Compute max value
max_y <- max(sector_plot_data$jobs, na.rm = TRUE)

# Plot
p_sector <- ggplot(
  sector_plot_data,
  aes(x = year, y = jobs, colour = industries)
) +
  
  # COVID shading
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = 2019.8,
    xmax = 2021.2,
    ymin = -Inf,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = "grey70",
    alpha = 0.35
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2019, linetype = "dashed", colour = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2021, linetype = "dashed", colour = "darkgreen") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2023, linetype = "dashed", colour = "purple") +
  
  # Labels (FIXED positions)
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2019,
    y = max_y * 1.005,
    label = "ULEZ introduced",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2021,
    y = max_y * 1.005,
    label = "ULEZ Inner EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3,
    colour = "darkgreen"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2022.7,   # ✅ FIXED (was 2023.1)
    y = max_y * 1.005,
    label = "ULEZ Greater EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3,
    colour = "purple"
  ) +
  
  # COVID label
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2020.5,
    y = max_y * 0.92,
    label = "COVID-19\n(2020–2021)",
    size = 3.8,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Lines
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  
  # Axis formatting 
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(2016, 2023, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.1))  
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Sectoral Workforce Trends in London (2016–2023)",
    subtitle = "Selected industries",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of Jobs",
    colour = "Industry"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",   # ✅ prevents squashing
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# Plotly output 
ggplotly(p_sector) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = "Sectoral Workforce Trends (2016–2023)",
      x = 0,
      xanchor = "left"
    ),
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 40, t = 120, b = 100)
  )
```


```{r}
#Environmental
no2_data <- tibble(
  year = c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024),
  no2 = c(53,51,48,42,34,32,31,28,24)
)





#interactive dashboard

max_y <- max(no2_data$no2, na.rm = TRUE)

p_no2 <- ggplot(no2_data, aes(x = year, y = no2)) +
  
  # COVID shading
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = 2019.8,
    xmax = 2021.2,
    ymin = -Inf,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = "grey70",
    alpha = 0.35
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2019
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2019,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2021 expansion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2021,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "darkgreen",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2021,
    y = max_y * 1.005,
    label = "ULEZ Inner EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "darkgreen"
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2023 expansion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2023,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "purple",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2023.1,
    y = max_y * 1.005,
    label = "ULEZ Greater EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "purple"
  ) +
  
  # NO2 line
  geom_line(colour = "#1f78b4", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "#1f78b4") +
  
  # ULEZ 2019 label
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2019,
    y = max_y * 0.92,
    label = "ULEZ introduced",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 4
  ) +
  
  # COVID label 
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2020.5,
    y = max_y * 0.85,
    label = "COVID-19 period",
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Annual Average NO₂ Concentrations",
    subtitle = "Measured concentrations (µg/m³)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "NO₂ concentration (µg/m³)",
    caption = "2024 data up to September"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggplotly(p_no2, tooltip = c("x", "y")) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = "Annual Average NO₂ Concentrations",
      x = 0,
      xanchor = "left"
    ),
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 40, t = 120, b = 80)
  )

```

```{r}
# PM2.5 data
pm25_data <- tibble(
  year = 2016:2024,
  pm25 = c(15.0, 13.3, 13.2, 12.6, 10.4, 10.2, 10.5, 9.2, 8.3)
)

max_y_pm <- max(pm25_data$pm25, na.rm = TRUE)

p_pm25 <- ggplot(pm25_data, aes(x = year, y = pm25)) +
  
  # COVID shading
  annotate(
    "rect",
    xmin = 2019.8,
    xmax = 2021.2,
    ymin = -Inf,
    ymax = Inf,
    fill = "grey70",
    alpha = 0.35
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2019
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2019,
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2021 expansion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2021,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "darkgreen",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2021,
    y = max_y_pm * 1.02,
    label = "ULEZ Inner EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "darkgreen"
  ) +
  
  # ULEZ 2023 expansion
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 2023,
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "purple",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2023.1,
    y = max_y_pm * 1.02,
    label = "ULEZ Greater EXP",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.4,
    colour = "purple"
  ) +
  
  # PM2.5 line
  geom_line(colour = "#1f78b4", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, colour = "#1f78b4") +
  
  # ULEZ 2019 label
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2019,
    y = max_y_pm * 0.92,
    label = "ULEZ introduced",
    angle = 90,
    vjust = -0.5,
    size = 4
  ) +
  
  # COVID label
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = 2020.5,
    y = max_y_pm * 0.85,
    label = "COVID-19 period",
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Annual Average PM2.5 Concentrations",
    subtitle = "Measured concentrations (µg/m³)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "PM2.5 concentration (µg/m³)",
    caption = "2024 data up to September"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

ggplotly(p_pm25, tooltip = c("x", "y")) %>%
  layout(
    title = list(
      text = "Annual Average PM2.5 Concentrations",
      x = 0,
      xanchor = "left"
    ),
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 40, t = 120, b = 80)
  )

```


---

# **Economic Analysis**

Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Active Enterprises in London

```{r}
ggplotly(p_active)

```

### Business Births vs Deaths
```{r}
ggplotly(p_birth_death)


```


Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### Job Density
```{r}
ggplotly(p_job_density)

```

### Workforce
```{r}
ggplotly(p_workforce)

```

### Sector
```{r}
ggplotly(p_sector)

```

---

# **Environmental Analysis**

Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### no2
```{r}
ggplotly(p_no2)
```
Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### pm25
```{r}
ggplotly(p_pm25)
```


```{r}
# 3D SURFACE DATA
sector_wide <- workforce_sector_filtered %>%
  select(year, industries, jobs) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = industries, values_from = jobs)

z_matrix <- as.matrix(sector_wide[ , -1])

p_surface3d <- plot_ly(
  x = sector_wide$year,
  y = seq_len(ncol(z_matrix)),
  z = z_matrix,
  type = "surface",
  colorscale = "Viridis"
) %>%
  layout(
    title = "3D Surface Model: Workforce Jobs by Sector (2016–2023)",
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(title = "Year"),
      yaxis = list(
        title = "Sector",
        tickvals = seq_len(ncol(z_matrix)),
        ticktext = colnames(z_matrix)
      ),
      zaxis = list(title = "Jobs")
    )
  )

```


Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
```{r}
# 3D SCATTER DATA
p_birthdeath3d <- plot_ly(
  business_summary,
  x = ~year,
  y = ~total_births,
  z = ~total_deaths,
  type = "scatter3d",
  mode = "lines+markers",
  marker = list(size = 4)
) %>%
  layout(
    title = "3D Model: Year vs Business Births vs Business Deaths",
    scene = list(
      xaxis = list(title = "Year"),
      yaxis = list(title = "Business Births"),
      zaxis = list(title = "Business Deaths")
    )
  )

```

# **3D Graphs**
Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### 3D Workforce Jobs Surface Plot
```{r}
p_surface3d
```
Column {data-width=1000}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
### 3D births/deaths Plot
```{r}
p_birthdeath3d

```
